Call for Papers

ISORC 2027 Call for Papers

We invite high-quality submissions on real-time distributed computing — from foundational methods and architectures to evaluated systems, applications, and industrial experience reports.

Submission Guidelines

Tracks

Conference Tracks

Final track names, page limits, and deadlines are subject to approval by the ISORC 2027 organizing committee.

Research Track

Original research contributions on real-time distributed systems, architectures, algorithms, middleware, platforms, and evaluation.

Industry & Practitioner Track

Practical reports, industrial deployments, lessons learned, tools, and validated systems in real-world settings.

Posters & Demos

Interactive presentations of early-stage results, prototypes, tools, datasets, and demonstrable systems.

Doctoral Symposium

Mentoring-focused venue for PhD researchers working on emerging problems in real-time distributed computing.

Dissertation Digest

Concise presentation of recently completed dissertations relevant to ISORC's technical scope.

Workshops, Tutorials & Panels

Community sessions on focused research themes, emerging practices, education, and open technical debates.

Track Details

Submission tracks

ISORC 2027 will include the following submission tracks, each with dedicated review and presentation formats.

  • Research Track: Full papers reporting novel contributions, system design, evaluation, or theory in real-time distributed computing.
  • Poster Track: Work-in-progress reports, emerging ideas, demos, and early-stage research discussions.
  • Doctoral Symposium & Dissertation Digests: PhD research summaries, dissertation digests, and early-career investigator presentations.

Camera Ready Instructions

Accepted papers will receive camera-ready formatting instructions that include the IEEE template, copyright forms, and final submission deadlines.

Instructions will be added once the review process is complete.

Topics of Interest

Research Scope

Topics include, but are not limited to, the following areas:

  • Hardware and software architectures for real-time distributed computing
  • Object-, component-, microservice-, and service-oriented real-time systems
  • Distributed real-time communication, protocols, middleware, and time-sensitive networking
  • Cyber-physical systems, IoT, edge, fog, and cloud-edge continuum platforms
  • Real-time operating systems, virtualization, containerization, orchestration, and resource management
  • Scheduling, synchronization, timing analysis, QoS management, and performance engineering
  • Dependability, fault tolerance, resilience, safety, security, and formal verification
  • Real-time analytics, stream processing, digital twins, federated learning, TinyML, and edge AI
  • System evaluation, benchmarking, observability, field studies, and reproducible experimentation
  • Applications in transportation, aerospace, medical devices, Industry 4.0, smart grids, robotics, and autonomous systems

CFP Status

The official ISORC 2027 CFP PDF and submission portal will be published after approval by the organizing committee.

To be announced

ISORC 2027

30th IEEE International Symposium on Real-Time Distributed Computing

April 2–4, 2027 · Indian Institute of Technology Ropar, India

Host

Department of Computer Science and Engineering, IIT Ropar

Indian Institute of Technology Ropar, Punjab, India
